cases.query: How has the transition to the Unity Catalog model registry affected your workflow?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a reliable migration tool that preserves line
As a Data Engineer, I want a more effective bulk migration tool for moving table

pain_points.description: The transition to the Unity Catalog model registry disrupted CI/CD tooling due to changes in model URI format.
Loss of lineage information during migration to Unity Catalog, impacting compliance documentation.
Migration from old Hive metastore to Unity Catalog is complex and time-consuming, with inadequate tooling for bulk migrations.
Column-level tags and custom properties do not carry over during migration, requiring manual re-tagging.
Gaps in lineage data for certain operations limit the ability to track data flow for compliance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a reliable migration tool that preserves lineage information during the transition to Unity Catalog, so that I can maintain compliance documentation without manual reconstruction.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of losing important lineage data during migration, which is crucial for compliance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a more effective bulk migration tool for moving tables to Unity Catalog, so that I can reduce the time and effort required for migration.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the complexity and time consumption of the current migration process.

cases.summary: The transition to the Unity Catalog model registry has caused significant disruptions in workflows, particularly affecting CI/CD tooling and lineage tracking. Users are facing challenges with migration complexity and inadequate tooling, leading to increased manual effort and potential compliance risks.

cases.query: Can you describe any performance issues you're facing when using Databricks SQL?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex mo
As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or budget alerts for serverless ware
As a Data Analyst, I want to have stable connections when using Tableau with Dat
As an Analytics Engineer, I want dbt metadata to automatically propagate to Unit
As an Analytics Engineer, I want visibility into the reasons for query latency v

pain_points.description: Long query compilation time on complex models with multiple CTEs, causing delays in development iteration.
Unpredictable cost due to serverless warehouse scaling, leading to unexpected expenses.
Frequent disconnections when using Tableau with Databricks SQL, impacting user experience.
Lack of automatic propagation of dbt metadata to Unity Catalog, requiring manual syncing.
Significant and unpredictable query latency variance, with no visibility into performance issues.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models, so that I can iterate faster during development.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of long compilation times that slow down development iterations.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or budget alerts for serverless warehouses, so that I can manage and predict expenses effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the unpredictability of costs associated with serverless warehouse scaling.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to have stable connections when using Tableau with Databricks SQL, so that I can work without interruptions.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frequent disconnections that impact the user experience.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want dbt metadata to automatically propagate to Unity Catalog, so that I can maintain accurate documentation without manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the inconvenience of manually syncing dbt metadata to Unity Catalog.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want visibility into the reasons for query latency variance, so that I can diagnose and improve performance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the lack of visibility into query performance issues, which is a significant pain point.

cases.summary: Users are experiencing significant performance issues with Databricks SQL, particularly around long query compilation times and unpredictable query latency. Additionally, there are concerns regarding cost management with serverless warehouses and connectivity issues with BI tools like Tableau. Addressing these pain points could greatly enhance user experience and efficiency.

cases.query: How do analysts feel about the performance of their queries?

per_story.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language performance summary for my queries, s
As an Analytics Engineer, I want a visual timeline breakdown of query execution

pain_points.description: Query performance variance leads to unpredictable execution times, causing frustration and lack of trust in results.
Analysts lack visibility into the reasons for slow queries and do not have a self-service path to diagnose performance issues.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language performance summary for my queries, so that I can understand why they are slow and how to improve them.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for analysts to gain insights into query performance without needing to interpret complex Spark plans.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want a visual timeline breakdown of query execution phases, so that I can quickly identify performance bottlenecks.
user_stories.rationale: This story helps engineers diagnose issues efficiently, addressing the pain point of unpredictable query performance.

cases.summary: Analysts express significant frustration with unpredictable query performance in Databricks SQL, often experiencing wide variances in execution times. There is a strong need for tools that provide clear explanations of query performance issues and actionable recommendations to improve efficiency.

cases.query: What are the key pain points in cost management for MLflow artifacts?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define artifact retention policies for MLflow exp

pain_points.description: Artifact storage costs are growing unchecked due to abandoned experiments and lack of retention policies.
There is no native tooling in MLflow to define or enforce retention policies, leading to indefinite accumulation of artifacts.
Bulk deletion of experiments is cumbersome as it requires API iteration without a UI option for bulk operations.
Storage costs for MLflow artifacts are not clearly attributed to experiments in Databricks cost reporting.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define artifact retention policies for MLflow experiments, so that I can automatically clean up stale artifacts and reduce storage costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for managing artifact storage costs and preventing indefinite accumulation of unused artifacts.

cases.summary: The key pain points in cost management for MLflow artifacts include unchecked storage costs due to abandoned experiments and the absence of native retention policies. Users face challenges with bulk deletion and lack of cost attribution in reporting, highlighting the need for improved management tools within MLflow.

cases.query: What limitations are users experiencing with serverless compute in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive cost caps or budget alerts at the warehous
As a Data Scientist, I want to have the ability to install packages with system-
As a Data Engineer, I want built-in stream health alerting for Auto Loader strea

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models can take 20-30 seconds before any data is processed, slowing down development iteration.
Cost unpredictability with serverless warehouses leads to unexpected expenses due to automatic scaling.
Environment management in serverless compute is limited, as users cannot install packages requiring system-level dependencies and the package set resets every session.
Lack of built-in stream health alerting for Auto Loader streams can result in unnoticed data stalling.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive cost caps or budget alerts at the warehouse level, so that I can manage serverless compute expenses more predictably.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of cost unpredictability with serverless warehouses.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to have the ability to install packages with system-level dependencies in serverless compute, so that I can utilize all necessary libraries for my work.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the limitation of environment management in serverless compute.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want built-in stream health alerting for Auto Loader streams, so that I can be notified of any data processing issues immediately.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of unnoticed data stalling in streams.

cases.summary: Users are experiencing significant limitations with serverless compute in Databricks, including long query compilation times, cost unpredictability, and restrictions on package management. Additionally, the lack of stream health alerting can lead to unnoticed data processing issues, impacting overall workflow efficiency.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to DLT pipeline performance and monitoring?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform partial refreshes on DLT pipelines, so tha
As a Data Engineer, I want local testing capabilities for DLT expectations and t
As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and detailed diagnostics in DL
As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation and monitoring for watermarking

pain_points.description: Full refresh of DLT pipelines drops and recreates all tables, causing long reprocessing times and slowing down updates.
Testing changes in DLT is expensive and lacks local testing capabilities, requiring cloud compute for every test.
Error messages in DLT are often generic and insufficient for diagnosing failures, leading to reliance on support tickets.
Watermarking and late data handling in DLT is poorly documented, leading to silent data drops without warnings.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform partial refreshes on DLT pipelines, so that I can update specific tables without lengthy reprocessing times.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of long reprocessing times caused by full refreshes, enabling faster iterations.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want local testing capabilities for DLT expectations and transformations, so that I can validate changes without incurring cloud compute costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of expensive testing processes that currently require cloud resources.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and detailed diagnostics in DLT event logs, so that I can quickly identify the root cause of pipeline failures without opening support tickets.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of generic error messages that hinder self-diagnosis and increase dependency on support.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation and monitoring for watermarking and late data handling in DLT, so that I can avoid silent data drops and ensure data integrity.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of unnoticed data discrepancies due to poor documentation and monitoring.

cases.summary: The key pain points related to DLT pipeline performance and monitoring include lengthy reprocessing times due to full refreshes, expensive testing processes, insufficient error diagnostics, and poor documentation on watermarking and late data handling. Addressing these issues through targeted user stories can significantly enhance the efficiency and reliability of DLT pipelines.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have regarding the lineage graph in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all work
As a Platform Admin, I want to run impact analysis from any table or column node

pain_points.description: The lineage graph stops at workspace boundaries, preventing users from tracing data lineage across multiple workspaces.
Column-level lineage is not captured for all operation types, leading to incomplete lineage tracking.
There is no automated way to perform blast-radius analysis for upstream schema changes, making it difficult to assess impacts on downstream data.
Data stewards struggle to answer compliance questions about data flow without manual investigation across multiple workspaces.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all workspaces in the account, so that I can assess the blast radius of upstream schema changes and answer compliance questions about data flow.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of lineage graph limitations that stop at workspace boundaries, enabling better data governance.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to run impact analysis from any table or column node in the lineage graph, so that I can quickly identify all downstream tables, views, and dashboards affected by changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the lack of automated blast-radius analysis, which is crucial for understanding the impact of schema changes.

cases.summary: Users have highlighted significant pain points regarding the lineage graph in Databricks, particularly its inability to trace lineage across multiple workspaces and the lack of automated impact analysis. Addressing these issues through specific user stories can enhance data governance and compliance capabilities for Platform Admins.

cases.query: How does the privilege model in Unity Catalog affect your team’s workflow?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a more robust migration tool for Unity Catalog that h
As a Senior Platform Engineer, I want stronger metadata isolation features in Un

pain_points.description: The migration from Hive metastore to Unity Catalog is challenging due to immature tooling for bulk migrations, leading to manual re-tagging of tables and validation queries.
Unity Catalog does not guarantee metadata isolation, which raises concerns about potential metadata leakage in environments with strict data isolation requirements.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a more robust migration tool for Unity Catalog that handles edge cases like column-level tags, so that I can reduce the manual effort required during the migration process.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of extensive manual work required due to current migration limitations.

user_stories.story: As a Senior Platform Engineer, I want stronger metadata isolation features in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure compliance with strict data isolation requirements for multi-client environments.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the concern of potential metadata leakage that could compromise client data security.

cases.summary: The migration to Unity Catalog presents significant challenges, particularly with the need for improved tooling to handle edge cases and reduce manual work. Additionally, there are critical concerns regarding metadata isolation that could impact compliance in multi-client environments.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to output quality evaluation in the Mosaic AI Playground?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to define evaluation rubrics and rate outputs inline i
As an ML Engineer, I want to log agent evaluation results to MLflow alongside mo

pain_points.description: Output quality evaluation is manual, requiring engineers to copy-paste outputs into a spreadsheet and rate them subjectively with no statistical tracking.
There is no systematic way to evaluate agent quality beyond manual spot-checking outputs.
Agent evaluation results are not tracked in MLflow alongside model metrics, leading to a lack of unified quality view.
Ground truth dataset management is ad hoc, with evaluation sets typically stored as CSV files in notebooks with no versioning or governance.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to define evaluation rubrics and rate outputs inline in the Mosaic AI Playground, so that I can track output quality statistically across different prompt versions.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual evaluation and lack of statistical tracking by providing a structured way to evaluate outputs.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to log agent evaluation results to MLflow alongside model metrics, so that I can have a unified view of quality across both classical ML models and LLM agents.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of not having a unified quality view by integrating evaluation results into MLflow.

cases.summary: Key pain points in the Mosaic AI Playground include the manual nature of output quality evaluation and the lack of systematic tracking for agent quality. There is also a need for better management of ground truth datasets and integration of evaluation results into MLflow for a unified view of performance metrics.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations Analytics Engineers face with serverless warehouses?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex db
As an Analytics Engineer, I want to set cost caps or receive budget alerts for s
As an Analytics Engineer, I want to improve query performance consistency, so th

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models is too long, causing delays in development iteration.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex dbt models, so that I can iterate faster during development.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of long delays in seeing results after making changes to models.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to set cost caps or receive budget alerts for serverless warehouses, so that I can manage expenses more predictably.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the concern of unexpected costs due to automatic scaling of serverless warehouses.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to improve query performance consistency, so that analysts can trust their results and time estimates.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have for improving data governance in Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descri
As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce a tag taxonomy for feature tables in Unit
As a Data Scientist, I want a 'similar feature' suggestion when creating a new f

pain_points.description: The current Unity Catalog search only matches table names, not column names, descriptions, or tags, leading to duplicate feature creation.
Feature tables in Unity Catalog lack enforced description or tag taxonomy, resulting in empty descriptions and ineffective text search.
Teams spend 15–20% of feature engineering time rebuilding features that already exist, leading to wasted effort.
There is no 'similar feature' suggestion when creating a new feature definition, increasing the risk of duplicates.
Lineage tracking has gaps for certain operations, making it difficult to trace data governance accurately.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descriptions, and tags in Unity Catalog, so that I can find relevant existing features in under 2 minutes before building a new one.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of duplicate feature creation by enabling efficient feature discovery.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce a tag taxonomy for feature tables in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure all features are properly documented and searchable.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of empty descriptions and ineffective searches by requiring structured metadata.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want a 'similar feature' suggestion when creating a new feature definition, so that I can avoid creating duplicates.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly addresses the pain of redundant feature engineering by alerting users to existing features.

cases.summary: Users have highlighted significant pain points in data governance within Unity Catalog, particularly around feature discoverability and duplicate feature creation. Key feature requests include enhanced search capabilities, enforced tagging, and duplicate detection to streamline feature engineering and improve compliance.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding model promotion documentation?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Mode

pain_points.description: Current model promotion process relies on Slack messages and lacks a formal approval workflow, leaving no traceable audit record.
Any user with EXECUTE privilege can promote models to Production without any required reviewer or approval process.
Promotion events lack detailed audit information regarding reviews, approvers, and criteria evaluated.
The Model Registry does not have a workflow engine to connect lifecycle stages, making it difficult to enforce governance.
Teams in regulated industries are hesitant to adopt Databricks due to the inability to demonstrate model governance to auditors.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Model Registry, so that I can ensure all model promotions are reviewed and documented for compliance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a formal approval process that meets regulatory requirements and provides an auditable record.

cases.summary: Users in regulated industries face significant challenges with the current model promotion process, which lacks formal approval workflows and audit trails. This leads to compliance risks and hinders adoption of Databricks. Implementing a structured approval process in the Model Registry is critical for meeting regulatory standards and ensuring model governance.

cases.query: How can Databricks improve the alerting system for job monitoring?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to configure SLA alerting on Auto Loader streams nati
As a Data Engineer, I want to receive alerts if a job hasn't started by a specif
As a Data Engineer, I want an event-driven alert mechanism for stalled streaming
As a Platform Admin, I want a native SLA dashboard for all pipelines, so that I

pain_points.description: Setting up alerting for Auto Loader streams takes excessive engineering time and can fail silently.
Current alerting only notifies on job failure, lacking alerts for jobs that haven't started or are delayed.
No event-driven alert mechanism exists for stalled streaming pipelines, requiring manual polling.
Multi-pipeline SLA dashboards must be built manually, leading to redundancy and inefficiency.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to configure SLA alerting on Auto Loader streams natively, so that I can reduce setup time and avoid silent failures in monitoring.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of excessive engineering time and the fragility of current monitoring solutions.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive alerts if a job hasn't started by a specified time, so that I can proactively manage job scheduling and avoid delays.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for more comprehensive alerting beyond just job failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want an event-driven alert mechanism for stalled streaming pipelines, so that I can quickly respond to issues without manual checks.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of relying on manual polling for monitoring streaming pipeline health.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a native SLA dashboard for all pipelines, so that I can easily monitor compliance and status without redundant setups.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the inefficiency of manually creating dashboards for SLA monitoring.

cases.summary: The current alerting system for job monitoring in Databricks has significant pain points, including excessive engineering time for setup and lack of comprehensive alerts. Proposed user stories focus on enhancing SLA configurations and introducing event-driven alerts to improve monitoring efficiency and reliability.

cases.query: What feature requests do Data Engineers have for improving error diagnostics in DLT?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries i
As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after p

pain_points.description: Failure diagnosis requires opening a support ticket and waiting hours for a response due to insufficient error information in the pipeline event log.
DLT executor logs are not persisted after cluster termination, making it impossible to investigate failures after the fact.
Generic runtime error messages do not provide specific details about the cause of failures, leading to confusion and delays.
The DLT event log lacks original exception class, stack trace, or offending record, making root cause analysis difficult.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly identify the root cause of pipeline failures without opening a support ticket.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for actionable error diagnostics to reduce downtime and improve trust in pipeline outputs.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after pipeline termination, so that I can investigate failures even after the cluster has stopped.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of losing critical logs that are necessary for diagnosing issues post-failure.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with error diagnostics in DLT due to insufficient error information, lack of persisted logs, and generic error messages. Structured error codes and log persistence are critical feature requests to enable self-diagnosis and reduce reliance on support tickets.

cases.query: What issues did the attendees highlight about the SCIM synchronization process?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want the SCIM synchronization process to reliably deactiv

pain_points.description: The SCIM sync fails intermittently, causing deprovisioned users to retain access for 4-6 hours due to internal server errors.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want the SCIM synchronization process to reliably deactivate users within 1 hour of their Okta account deactivation, so that I can ensure compliance with SOC 2 Type II requirements and maintain security.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for timely user deactivation to prevent security incidents and compliance issues.

cases.summary: Attendees highlighted significant issues with the SCIM synchronization process, particularly its failure to promptly deactivate users after they are removed from Okta, which poses security risks. The need for a reliable solution is underscored by compliance requirements and past security incidents.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have to improve their experience with notebook environment management?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to edit a notebook simultaneously with my teammates
As a Data Scientist, I want to resume my notebook session with all packages and

pain_points.description: Users face silent overwrite risks and lack of collaboration features in notebooks, leading to inefficiencies.
Cluster terminations wipe all in-memory state, requiring users to manually reinstall packages and reload datasets each session.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to edit a notebook simultaneously with my teammates with real-time collaboration features, so that we can work together without losing our changes or encountering merge conflicts.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for collaborative work without the risk of overwriting each other's contributions.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to resume my notebook session with all packages and data restored, so that I can start working immediately without manual setup each day.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of losing in-memory state due to cluster terminations and the time wasted on reinstallation.

cases.summary: Users have expressed a strong need for real-time collaboration features in notebooks to enhance teamwork and reduce the risk of overwriting work. Additionally, the ability to persist notebook environments and restore them seamlessly is crucial to improve efficiency and minimize downtime caused by cluster terminations.

cases.query: Is there a way to log schema changes in Auto Loader without failing?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to pause the stream on breaking schema ch

pain_points.description: Non-additive schema changes cause stream failures with minimal diagnostic information.
There is no proactive alerting for breaking schema changes, leading to unplanned downtime.
The schema evolution mode is all-or-nothing, failing on any breaking change without a graduated handling option.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to pause the stream on breaking schema changes and log the details, so that I can resolve issues without experiencing stream failures.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better handling of schema changes to prevent downtime and improve diagnostics.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with Auto Loader due to unhandled non-additive schema changes that lead to stream failures and lack of proactive alerts. The current schema evolution mode does not provide a graduated response to breaking changes, necessitating improvements to prevent downtime and enhance operational efficiency.

cases.query: What user needs are unmet regarding production LLM application quality?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an evaluation harness for LLM agents that integrates w
As an ML Engineer, I want to log the system prompt version and model endpoint ID

cases.query: What challenges did Amara face when updating cluster policies across multiple workspaces?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define cluster policies in the Account Console an

pain_points.description: Cluster policy updates must be applied to each workspace individually, requiring multiple edits for each policy change.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define cluster policies in the Account Console and push them to selected workspaces, so that I can update policies across multiple workspaces in a single operation and save time.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of having to make individual updates to each workspace, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

cases.summary: Amara faced significant challenges when updating cluster policies across multiple workspaces due to the lack of a centralized management system, requiring individual updates for each workspace. This inefficiency leads to increased administrative overhead and time consumption for Platform Admins managing multiple workspaces.

cases.query: What challenges do data professionals encounter with automated classification of PII/PHI?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want an automated classifier in Unity Catalog that suggest
As a Platform Admin, I want policy templates in Unity Catalog that automatically
As a Data Engineer, I want Unity Catalog to automatically flag new columns in ta

pain_points.description: Manual tagging and policy enforcement for PHI/PII columns, leading to inefficiencies and potential compliance risks.
Initial classification of tables and columns is entirely manual, with no automated suggestions for PII candidates.
Tag-based policy enforcement requires custom definitions per table, lacking automatic application of access policies based on tags.
Re-classification of columns after schema changes is manual, risking oversight of new sensitive data.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want an automated classifier in Unity Catalog that suggests PII/PHI classifications, so that I can reduce the manual effort required for initial classification of sensitive columns.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual classification and the need for efficiency in managing sensitive data.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want policy templates in Unity Catalog that automatically apply access policies based on classification tags, so that I can ensure compliance without building custom enforcement logic.
user_stories.rationale: This story tackles the challenge of manual policy enforcement and enhances compliance management.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Unity Catalog to automatically flag new columns in tagged tables for classification review, so that I can ensure new sensitive data is evaluated promptly.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual re-classification after schema changes, helping to maintain data governance.

cases.summary: Data professionals face significant challenges with the manual processes required for classifying and enforcing policies on PII/PHI data in Databricks. The lack of automation in classification and policy enforcement leads to inefficiencies and compliance risks. User stories highlight the need for automated classification, policy templates, and change detection to streamline these processes.

cases.query: What frustrations do data scientists face when using the Feature Store for feature discovery?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want a robust search functionality in the Feature Store t

As a Data Scientist, I want native monitoring capabilities in the Feature Store

pain_points.description: Feature discoverability is poor, leading to duplicated feature definitions.
Inconsistent use of tags and lack of enforced taxonomy hampers effective search.
No built-in validation for data leakage in point-in-time joins.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want a robust search functionality in the Feature Store that allows searching by feature semantics and tags, so that I can easily discover existing features and avoid duplication.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical issue of data leakage that can lead to significant model performance degradation.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want native monitoring capabilities in the Feature Store for feature distribution shifts, so that I can receive alerts and respond to data drift proactively.

Improvements in these areas could greatly enhance the usability and effectiveness of the Feature Store.

cases.query: What overall stability issues does Mei-Ling report with long-running training jobs on Databricks?

pain_points.description: Occasional random cluster failures during long-running training jobs without clear explanations.
Cluster startup latency for GPU jobs causing significant overhead before training begins.
Ineffective handling of spot instance preemptions leading to job failures without checkpointing.
MLflow artifact logging lacks resumable upload support, causing failures on large uploads.

user_stories.story: As a Senior ML Engineer, I want to receive detailed notifications when a cluster fails unexpectedly, so that I can understand the cause and prevent future occurrences.

user_stories.story: As a Senior ML Engineer, I want to reduce the cluster startup latency for GPU jobs, so that I can minimize the overhead before training begins.

cases.summary: Mei-Ling reports significant stability issues with long-running training jobs on Databricks, including random cluster failures and high startup latencies for GPU jobs. cases.query: What feature requests do ML engineers have for better evaluating LLM agents?

As an ML Engineer, I want built-in LLM-as-judge scorers for various evaluation m
As an ML Engineer, I want to track agent evaluation results in MLflow alongside
As an ML Engineer, I want versioned ground truth datasets registered as Unity Ca

pain_points.description: ML engineers lack a systematic way to evaluate agent quality, relying on manual spot-checking of outputs.
There is no first-class evaluation harness in Databricks for agent workloads, requiring boilerplate setup for LLM-as-judge evaluation patterns.
Agent evaluation results are not tracked in MLflow alongside model metrics, leading to a lack of unified quality view.
Regression detection is entirely manual, requiring engineers to remember to re-run evaluations after each agent change.
Ground truth dataset management is ad hoc, with evaluation sets typically stored as CSV files in notebooks without versioning.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an mlflow.evaluate()-compatible API for agent workloads, so that I can measure agent quality quantitatively and log results automatically.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of lacking a systematic evaluation method by providing a structured API for logging evaluation results.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want built-in LLM-as-judge scorers for various evaluation metrics, so that I can automate the scoring process and reduce manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual scoring by introducing automated scoring mechanisms that streamline the evaluation process.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to track agent evaluation results in MLflow alongside model metrics, so that I can have a unified view of quality across models.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of fragmented evaluation tracking by integrating agent evaluation results with existing MLflow metrics.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want versioned ground truth datasets registered as Unity Catalog tables, so that I can ensure consistent evaluation across different runs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of ad hoc dataset management by providing a structured and versioned approach to handling evaluation datasets.

cases.summary: ML engineers face significant challenges in evaluating LLM agents, primarily due to the lack of systematic evaluation methods and integration with existing tools like MLflow. Key feature requests include an evaluation API, built-in scoring mechanisms, and improved dataset management to streamline the evaluation process and enhance quality tracking.

cases.query: What unmet needs do Data Engineers have when dealing with Delta Live Tables errors?

cases.summary: Analysts are frustrated with unpredictable query performance and lack of visibility into the reasons behind slow queries. The need for plain-language explanations and detailed performance breakdowns is critical for enabling analysts to optimize their queries effectively.

cases.query: What are the frustrations users face with the current DLT pipeline refresh process?

cases.summary: Users face significant frustrations with the DLT pipeline refresh process, primarily due to the lengthy full refresh times that block downstream consumers and the complexity introduced by current workarounds. The lack of a selective refresh capability exacerbates these issues, leading to inefficient resource use and management challenges.

cases.summary: Users experience significant frustration with fine-tuning jobs due to failures caused by internal infrastructure errors and the absence of checkpointing, resulting in wasted compute resources. Clearer error messaging and the implementation of checkpointing are critical to improving user experience and job reliability.

cases.summary: Data Engineers are frustrated with the current error messages in DLT pipelines due to their cryptic nature and lack of actionable information. cases.summary: ML Engineers experience significant frustrations with the Feature Store, particularly related to point-in-time lookups that can cause data leakage. The lack of validation for timestamp columns is a critical pain point that can lead to severe consequences in model performance.

cases.query: What overall feedback did Nina provide about the direction of the Mosaic AI platform?

As an AI/ML Engineer, I want a centralized prompt management system integrated w

cases.query: What challenges do you face with memory limitations on personal compute clusters?

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges with memory limitations on personal compute clusters, often requiring multi-node setups for medium-scale analysis. cases.query: What unmet needs related to task-level retries in Workflows were mentioned?

pain_points.description: The retry logic in Workflows does not allow for task-level retries, causing the entire DAG to restart instead of just the failed task.

Data Engineers expressed frustration over the current retry logic that restarts the entire DAG instead of just the failed task, wasting valuable compute time.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations expressed by Data Engineers regarding DLT pipeline refreshes?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform a selective refresh on a single DLT table,

pain_points.description: A full refresh on a DLT pipeline takes 4-8 hours, blocking all downstream consumers and slowing down iteration speed.
Teams are forced to split large pipelines into many smaller ones to avoid long refresh times, leading to increased management overhead.
DLT has no concept of 'dirty' tables, making it impossible to skip unaffected tables during a refresh.
Testing changes in DLT is painful due to the lack of local testing capabilities and the need to keep a cluster running.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform a selective refresh on a single DLT table, so that I can reduce the refresh time from 8 hours to under 20% of that time and avoid blocking downstream consumers.

cases.summary: Data Engineers express significant frustration with the lengthy full refresh times required for DLT pipelines, which can take 4-8 hours and block downstream consumers. To mitigate this, teams resort to splitting pipelines into smaller units, increasing management complexity. Additionally, the lack of a mechanism to identify and refresh only 'dirty' tables exacerbates the issue, highlighting the need for a selective refresh feature.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want actionable recommendations linked to documentation for slow queries, so that I can implement improvements based on clear guidance.
user_stories.rationale: This story fulfills the need for users to have a clear path to resolving performance issues.

cases.summary: Users are facing significant challenges with unpredictable query performance and lack the tools to diagnose and improve it. The absence of detailed execution breakdowns and plain-language explanations exacerbates the issue, highlighting a critical need for enhanced performance insights and actionable guidance within Databricks SQL.

pain_points.description: No support for cross-workspace task dependencies in Databricks Workflows, leading to reliance on fragile polling workarounds.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have for improving cost visibility in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databrick
As an Analytics Engineer, I want to set per-warehouse budget caps, so that I can
As an Analyst, I want to see an estimated DBU cost for my queries before executi

Lack of per-warehouse budget caps leads to unpredictable costs from serverless warehouses.

cases.query: Can you summarize the key pain points around conditional branching implementation?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to implement conditional task execution without relyi
As a Data Engineer, I want conditional skip events to be logged with the evaluat

pain_points.description: Conditional branching relies on undocumented notebook exit values, leading to fragile implementations that can break silently.
The current implementation of conditional skips does not log the evaluated condition or its result, making it difficult to understand why tasks were skipped.
There is no support for dynamic parallelism in task execution, limiting the ability to process variable-length outputs efficiently.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to implement conditional task execution without relying on notebook exit values, so that I can create robust workflows that are easy to maintain.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of fragile implementations that can break with changes, ensuring a more stable workflow environment.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional skip events to be logged with the evaluated condition expression, so that I can understand the reasons for task execution paths in my workflows.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for visibility into task execution decisions, improving debugging and workflow management.

cases.summary: The key pain points around conditional branching implementation in Databricks Workflows include reliance on fragile notebook exit values and lack of visibility into conditional skips. These issues hinder the ability to create robust and maintainable workflows. Addressing these pain points through structured user stories can enhance the overall user experience for Data Engineers.

cases.query: What challenges do ML engineers face when evaluating their LLM-powered agents?

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges with real-time secret access in Databricks, primarily due to outdated secrets and the need for manual sync processes. These issues lead to security concerns and operational inefficiencies, highlighting the need for a more integrated and real-time solution for secret management.

cases.query: What is the current state of documentation regarding secret management in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want Databricks to fetch secret values live from HashiCor

pain_points.description: The integration between Databricks and HashiCorp Vault for secret management is one-directional and not real-time, leading to outdated secrets in Databricks.
The current sync process requires significant engineering time and can cause production incidents due to authentication errors when secrets are rotated.
Secret scope contents are not auditable in Vault's audit log because Databricks holds its own copy, complicating compliance and security audits.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want Databricks to fetch secret values live from HashiCorp Vault at runtime, so that I can ensure that rotated secrets take effect immediately without manual sync steps.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of outdated secrets causing authentication errors and security concerns.

cases.summary: The current state of documentation regarding secret management in Databricks reveals significant pain points related to the one-directional integration with HashiCorp Vault, leading to outdated secrets and compliance challenges. The need for real-time secret management is critical for security and operational efficiency.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations with cross-workspace governance in Databricks?

As a Platform Architect, I want a mechanism to discover all Databricks workspace

As a Platform Architect, I want native alerting capabilities on audit logs in Da

pain_points.description: No cross-workspace task dependencies in Databricks Workflows, leading to fragile polling workarounds.
Workspace sprawl with no mechanism for discovering all Databricks workspaces, posing governance risks.
Coarse privilege model at the account boundary in Unity Catalog, failing to enforce data domain isolation.
Overwhelming volume of audit logs without native alerting capabilities, complicating compliance management.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want Databricks Workflows to support cross-workspace task dependencies, so that I can eliminate fragile polling workarounds and reduce pipeline latency.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Architect, I want a mechanism to discover all Databricks workspaces automatically, so that I can ensure proper governance and compliance across our cloud accounts.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the risk of workspace sprawl and the potential for sensitive data to be unmanaged.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Architect, I want Unity Catalog to provide data domain isolation within a single metastore, so that I can enforce strict data access controls between different teams.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better data governance and privacy in a multi-team environment.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Architect, I want native alerting capabilities on audit logs in Databricks, so that I can quickly respond to unauthorized access attempts.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Architect, I want a central admin surface for managing Databricks workspaces, so that I can reduce operational overhead and improve efficiency.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of managing multiple isolated workspaces, which currently consumes excessive resources.

cases.summary: The main frustrations with cross-workspace governance in Databricks include the lack of support for task dependencies across workspaces, leading to inefficient workarounds, and the challenges posed by workspace sprawl and inadequate data governance mechanisms. Additionally, the operational overhead from managing multiple isolated workspaces and the overwhelming volume of audit logs without alerting capabilities further complicate governance efforts.

cases.query: What frustrations do Data Engineers have when handling schema changes in Auto Loader?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive alerts for breaking schema changes before
As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to provide detailed error messages that s
As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to have graduated handling for schema cha

pain_points.description: Non-additive schema changes (type changes, column renames, column deletions) cause stream failures with minimal diagnostic information.

The Auto Loader schema evolution modes do not provide graduated handling for breaking changes, forcing streams to fail without options for resolution.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive alerts for breaking schema changes before they cause stream failures, so that I can take proactive measures to resolve issues and minimize downtime.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for immediate awareness of upstream schema changes to prevent extended stream outages.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to provide detailed error messages that specify which column changed and what the old vs. new types are, so that I can quickly diagnose and fix schema issues.
user_stories.rationale: This story focuses on improving diagnostic information to reduce the time spent troubleshooting stream failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to have graduated handling for schema changes, so that I can manage new columns silently while being alerted and pausing on type changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the need for more flexible schema evolution handling to avoid unnecessary stream failures.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant frustrations with schema changes in Auto Loader, particularly with non-additive changes causing stream failures and a lack of proactive alerting. The current schema evolution modes do not allow for graduated handling of changes, leading to extended downtime and inefficient troubleshooting. Additionally, the accumulation of checkpoint files presents a cost management challenge.

Lack of proactive alerting for upstream schema changes results in engineers only learning about issues after a stream has failed.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to alert me and pause the stream on breaking schema changes, so that I can resolve issues before the stream fails.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for proactive handling of breaking changes to minimize downtime and improve operational efficiency.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a way to clean up checkpoint files in the schema location safely, so that I can manage storage costs without risking stream integrity.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of accumulating checkpoint files and the risks associated with manual deletion.

cases.summary: Users experience significant pain points with Auto Loader's handling of breaking schema changes, including stream failures with insufficient diagnostic information and lack of proactive alerts. Additionally, the accumulation of checkpoint files poses a management challenge. Addressing these issues through improved alerting and schema change handling is crucial for operational efficiency.

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to implement time-based cluster policies, so that I
As a Platform Admin, I want to set budget-triggered escalation rules for cluster
As a Platform Admin, I want a policy simulation tool, so that I can preview the

pain_points.description: Current cluster policies are static and do not allow for time-based or budget-based rules.
Cluster policies lack budget awareness, making it impossible to restrict usage after a certain spend threshold.
There is no policy simulation tool to preview the effects of policy changes before activation.
Updating cluster policies across multiple workspaces is a manual and cumbersome process.
Enforcing tagging for cost allocation is difficult due to the lack of native requirements for tags on resources.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to implement time-based cluster policies, so that I can restrict GPU access to the ML team during business hours only and reduce unnecessary costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for more expressive policies that adapt to usage patterns and help manage costs effectively.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to set budget-triggered escalation rules for cluster policies, so that I can automatically restrict resource usage after a team exceeds their budget.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of not being able to enforce cost governance effectively.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a policy simulation tool, so that I can preview the impact of proposed policy changes on past cluster usage before activating them.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better visibility and control over policy impacts, reducing the risk of unintended consequences.

cases.summary: Platform Admins face significant frustrations with static cluster policies that lack time-based and budget-aware rules, making cost management challenging. The absence of a policy simulation tool and the cumbersome process of updating policies across multiple workspaces further exacerbate these issues. Enhanced flexibility and control in cluster policy management are critical for effective cost governance.

cases.query: What pain points exist in documenting model promotion approvals for audits?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Mode

pain_points.description: Current model promotion process relies on Slack messages and spreadsheets, which do not provide a traceable audit record required by auditors.
Any user with EXECUTE privilege can promote a model to Production without any required review or approval, leading to potential compliance issues.
Promotion events are recorded in system.access.audit but lack information about the review process, approver identity, and evaluation criteria.
The Model Registry lacks a workflow engine to connect lifecycle stages, making it difficult to enforce a structured approval process.
Teams in regulated industries are hesitant to adopt Databricks due to the inability to demonstrate model governance to auditors.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Model Registry, so that I can ensure all model promotions are reviewed and documented for compliance audits.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a structured approval process that meets regulatory requirements and provides an auditable record.

cases.summary: The current model promotion process is inadequate for regulatory compliance, relying on informal methods that do not provide necessary audit trails. Key pain points include the lack of a structured approval workflow and the risk of unauthorized promotions, which hinder Databricks adoption in regulated industries.

cases.summary: ML engineers face significant challenges in evaluating LLM agent quality due to a lack of systematic tools and processes, leading to inefficiencies and potential quality regressions. cases.query: How did the team decide to address the security issue with user deprovisioning?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a reliable SCIM integration that ensures user access

cases.query: What frustrations do ML Engineers face when evaluating their RAG pipelines?

End-to-end latency of RAG pipelines is not tracked alongside quality metrics, causing teams to inadvertently optimize for quality at the expense of increased latency.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to run evaluations on my RAG pipeline with a unified evaluation harness, so that I can systematically measure the impact of configuration changes on retrieval and generation quality.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of not knowing if the pipeline is improving or deteriorating with changes made.

user_stories.rationale: This story tackles the issue of inconsistent evaluation due to poorly managed datasets.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to track end-to-end latency alongside quality metrics for my RAG pipeline, so that I can make informed decisions that balance quality and performance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for visibility into latency impacts when optimizing for quality.

cases.summary: ML Engineers face significant challenges in evaluating their RAG pipelines due to a lack of systematic evaluation methods, poor management of ground truth datasets, and insufficient tracking of latency metrics. Addressing these pain points through structured evaluation harnesses and better dataset management can enhance the quality and performance of RAG pipelines.

cases.query: What security concerns do users express about the current setup with Databricks and Vault?

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for enhanced security measures to protect sensitive client data in a multi-client environment.

cases.summary: Users express significant security concerns regarding the integration between Databricks and HashiCorp Vault, particularly the outdated secrets issue that can lead to operational incidents. As an ML Engineer, I want improved documentation on batch inference behavior for

pain_points.description: The MLflow UI for comparing experiments is basic and unwieldy for large numbers of runs and hyperparameters.
Batch inference with MLflow pyfunc models does not parallelize well, leading to performance issues due to model reinitialization.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an advanced experiment comparison view in MLflow, so that I can efficiently analyze multiple experiment runs without exporting data.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of unwieldy comparisons in the current MLflow UI, which hinders effective analysis.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want improved documentation on batch inference behavior for MLflow pyfunc models, so that I can optimize performance without relying on fragile workarounds.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the confusion and performance issues caused by unclear documentation on model loading behavior.

cases.summary: The ML Engineer expresses significant frustrations with the basic UI for experiment comparison in MLflow and performance issues with batch inference due to model reinitialization. These pain points highlight the need for enhanced features and clearer documentation to improve the user experience.

cases.query: What difficulties do analysts encounter while using the Databricks SQL editor?

per_story.story: As an Analyst, I want to collaborate on SQL queries in real-time, so that I can
As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex mo

pain_points.description: Lack of collaboration in the SQL editor leads to overwritten queries and data loss.
Long query compilation times for complex models slow down development iteration.
Unpredictable costs with serverless warehouses create stress for users.
JDBC connectivity issues with BI tools like Tableau lead to dropped connections.

user_stories.story: As an Analyst, I want to collaborate on SQL queries in real-time, so that I can avoid data loss and improve teamwork.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical issue of overwritten queries that can lead to significant data loss.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models, so that I can iterate faster during development.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the need for improved performance in query planning to enhance productivity.

cases.summary: Analysts face significant challenges with the Databricks SQL editor, including collaboration issues that can lead to data loss, long query compilation times that hinder development speed, and unpredictable costs associated with serverless warehouses. Additionally, connectivity problems with BI tools like Tableau further complicate their workflow.

cases.query: What feature requests can help improve the dashboard's reliability?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want a mechanism for dashboard stale-data detection,

pain_points.description: Dashboard refresh shows stale data despite successful refresh status, impacting trust in data accuracy.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want a mechanism for dashboard stale-data detection, so that I can catch stale data issues before they impact executive decision-making.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for immediate visibility into data freshness to prevent trust issues with the dashboard.

cases.summary: The primary pain point identified is the dashboard's failure to reflect current data despite successful refresh indications, leading to significant trust issues. A user story has been formulated to introduce a stale-data detection mechanism to enhance reliability and prevent future occurrences of this issue.

cases.query: What specific pain points did the Data Engineer identify due to the Jobs API v2.1 changes?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive notifications for breaking changes in the

pain_points.description: Breaking changes in the Jobs API v2.1 task dependency schema were not announced, leading to silent reconfiguration of jobs and data correctness issues.
Frequent breaking changes in the Jobs API schema cause Terraform configurations to break without clear deprecation notices.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive notifications for breaking changes in the Jobs API schema, so that I can proactively update my Terraform configurations and avoid production issues.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of unexpected job failures due to silent API changes.

cases.summary: Data Engineers are facing significant challenges due to unannounced breaking changes in the Jobs API v2.1, which have led to production failures and data correctness issues. The lack of clear communication regarding schema changes exacerbates the problem, making it difficult for engineers to maintain their Terraform configurations effectively.

cases.query: What frustrations do you have with Model Serving in Databricks?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to validate point-in-time joins to p
As a Data Analyst, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models in
As an ML Engineer, I want an advanced experiment comparison view in MLflow, so t

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models is excessively long, causing delays in development iteration.
The UI for comparing experiments in MLflow is basic and unwieldy, making it difficult to analyze multiple experiment runs efficiently.
The Feature Store does not validate point-in-time joins, leading to potential data leakage issues.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to validate point-in-time joins to prevent data leakage, so that I can ensure the integrity of my model training data.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for validation to avoid serious issues like data leakage that can affect model performance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models in Databricks SQL, so that I can iterate on my development faster and see results more quickly.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the frustration of long wait times that hinder productivity during model development.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an advanced experiment comparison view in MLflow, so that I can efficiently analyze multiple experiment runs without exporting data.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the inefficiency of the current MLflow UI, which complicates the analysis process.

cases.summary: Users experience significant frustrations with Model Serving in Databricks, particularly around long query compilation times and inadequate UI for experiment comparison. Additionally, the lack of validation in the Feature Store for point-in-time joins poses a serious risk of data leakage, impacting model integrity and performance.

cases.query: What specific changes are being proposed to reduce the need for user workarounds?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional task skips in Workflows to log the evalua
As a Data Engineer, I want explicit parameter validation in task execution, so t
As a Platform Admin, I want a clear deprecation process for the Jobs API Terrafo

pain_points.description: Conditional task skips in Workflows do not log the evaluated condition, making it hard to understand why tasks are skipped.
Silent fallback to default parameters in task types leads to unnoticed data issues.
Breaking changes in the Jobs API Terraform provider occur without clear communication, disrupting job configurations.
Lack of operational visibility leads to reliance on downstream impacts to identify issues, resulting in user-built workarounds.
Data teams frequently encounter the need for workarounds due to platform limitations, impacting productivity.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional task skips in Workflows to log the evaluated condition and its result, so that I can understand why tasks are skipped without ambiguity.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of invisible task skips that complicate debugging and understanding of workflows.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want explicit parameter validation in task execution, so that I can avoid silent fallbacks to default values that lead to incorrect data processing.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of unnoticed data issues caused by silent parameter fallbacks.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a clear deprecation process for the Jobs API Terraform provider, so that I can prevent silent breaking changes that disrupt job configurations.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of unexpected changes that break existing configurations, causing operational disruptions.

cases.summary: The key findings highlight significant pain points related to operational visibility and the need for clearer logging and validation in Databricks Workflows. Users are facing challenges with silent failures and breaking changes that lead to increased reliance on workarounds, indicating a need for improved platform stability and transparency.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have for improving cost management in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databrick
As a Data Engineer, I want to receive budget threshold alerts for my team tag, s

pain_points.description: Lack of detailed cost visibility by team, pipeline, or business unit, making it difficult to justify spending.
Manual effort required to create cost attribution dashboards, leading to inefficiencies and potential inaccuracies.
Inability to enforce tagging policies, resulting in unattributed spend and incomplete cost attribution.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databricks Account Console, so that I can produce a per-team cost breakdown for any 30-day window in under 5 minutes, with zero custom SQL or data exports required.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual cost reporting and the need for detailed visibility into spending.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive budget threshold alerts for my team tag, so that I can proactively manage our Databricks spending and avoid unexpected costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better cost management and helps prevent overspending.

cases.summary: Users are facing significant challenges with cost visibility and attribution in Databricks, leading to inefficiencies and difficulties in justifying expenses. The need for a comprehensive FinOps module and automated cost attribution features is critical to enhance cost management capabilities.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding query performance diagnostics?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want a visual timeline breakdown of query execution
As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language performance summary after each query,
As an Analytics Engineer, I want actionable recommendations linked to performanc

pain_points.description: Users experience significant and unpredictable query latency variance without visibility into the reasons for slow performance.
The current EXPLAIN output is a raw Spark physical plan that is incomprehensible to analytics engineers and analysts.
There is no in-product guidance connecting slow queries to actionable fixes, leaving users without a clear path to improve performance.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want a visual timeline breakdown of query execution phases, so that I can quickly identify where time is being spent and diagnose performance issues.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for visibility into query performance to understand latency variance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language performance summary after each query, so that I can understand the dominant factors affecting query speed without needing to interpret complex Spark plans.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly addresses the pain point of needing comprehensible explanations for slow queries.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want actionable recommendations linked to performance summaries, so that I can implement changes to improve query performance effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story fulfills the need for guidance on how to resolve slow query issues.

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges with query performance diagnostics, including unpredictable latency and a lack of clear explanations for slow queries. There is a strong demand for features that provide visual breakdowns of query execution, plain-language summaries, and actionable recommendations to improve performance.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of unwieldy comparisons in the MLflow UI, which currently hampers effective analysis.

cases.summary: The main pain points with MLflow's experiment tracking include a basic UI for experiment comparison and the lack of retention policies for abandoned experiments. cases.query: How does the lack of actionable information in the error logs impact Data Engineers?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and actionable diagnostics in

pain_points.description: Error messages in DLT pipelines are often cryptic and lack actionable information, leading to prolonged downtime.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and actionable diagnostics in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly identify and resolve pipeline failures without opening support tickets.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for immediate understanding of failure causes, reducing downtime and reliance on support.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges due to the lack of actionable information in DLT error logs, resulting in extended downtimes and reliance on support for issue resolution. The need for structured error diagnostics and persistent logs is critical to enhance self-diagnosis capabilities and improve operational efficiency.

cases.query: What improvements do users want for lineage graph functionalities in Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want cross-workspace lineage stitching in Unity Catalog,
As a Data Steward, I want automated blast-radius analysis in Unity Catalog, so t
As a Platform Admin, I want comprehensive column-level lineage capture for all o

There is no automated blast-radius analysis to identify all downstream dependencies affected by upstream changes.
Data stewards cannot easily answer compliance questions about data flow without manual investigation across multiple workspaces.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want cross-workspace lineage stitching in Unity Catalog, so that I can trace a column's complete lineage from raw source to BI dashboard across all workspaces.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of incomplete lineage tracing due to workspace boundaries.

user_stories.story: As a Data Steward, I want automated blast-radius analysis in Unity Catalog, so that I can quickly identify all downstream tables and dashboards affected by changes to a column.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for efficient impact analysis to manage data governance.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want comprehensive column-level lineage capture for all operation types in Unity Catalog, so that I can provide complete lineage information for compliance audits.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of gaps in lineage data that hinder compliance efforts.

cases.summary: Users want significant improvements to lineage graph functionalities in Unity Catalog, particularly the ability to trace lineage across multiple workspaces and capture column-level lineage for all operation types. Additionally, they seek automated blast-radius analysis to efficiently manage data governance and compliance reporting.

cases.query: What feature requests have users made to improve the notebook editing experience in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to edit a notebook simultaneously with my teammates
As a Data Scientist, I want a notebook-aware merge conflict resolution UI, so th

pain_points.description: Current notebook collaboration leads to silent overwrite risks and requires a 'one notebook, one person' rule to avoid merge conflicts.
Merge conflicts in notebooks are treated as JSON files, resulting in incomprehensible diffs that are difficult to resolve.
There is no presence system to see if colleagues are editing a notebook, leading to inefficient collaboration.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to edit a notebook simultaneously with my teammates while seeing each other's cursors, so that we can collaborate effectively without losing work.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for real-time collaboration to eliminate the 'one notebook, one person' rule and enhance teamwork.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want a notebook-aware merge conflict resolution UI, so that I can resolve conflicts at the cell level without dealing with JSON diffs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of resolving complex merge conflicts that currently take too much time and effort.

cases.summary: Users have expressed significant pain points regarding the lack of real-time collaboration features in Databricks notebooks, leading to inefficient workflows and cumbersome merge conflict resolutions. Key requests include the ability to edit notebooks simultaneously with visibility into each other's work and improved conflict resolution tools that understand notebook structure.

cases.query: What unmet needs do ML Engineers have when using the fine-tuning API?

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for traceability in production deployments to prevent and resolve issues effectively.

cases.summary: ML Engineers face significant challenges with the fine-tuning API, particularly around job failures without checkpoints and the need for manual evaluation setups. There is also a critical need for better version control of system prompts to diagnose production issues effectively.

cases.query: What improvements can be made to the handling of token expirations in Databricks?

cases.query: What frustrations do ML Engineers experience with the current prompt versioning in Mosaic AI Playground?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to automatically save and version prompts in the Mosai
As an ML Engineer, I want to compare outputs of the same prompt across multiple
As an ML Engineer, I want to integrate prompt experiments in the Playground with

pain_points.description: ML Engineers cannot track which prompt version is live in production, leading to potential quality drops.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to automatically save and version prompts in the Mosaic AI Playground, so that I can easily track which prompt version is live in production and avoid quality drops.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the disconnection between prompt management and model deployment tracking.

cases.summary: ML Engineers face significant frustrations with prompt versioning in the Mosaic AI Playground, primarily due to the inability to track live prompt versions and the ephemeral nature of prompt iterations. Additionally, the lack of integration with MLflow and the need for side-by-side model comparisons further complicate their workflow, impacting the quality and reliability of LLM applications.
